title: Fomo Kernel Prose-only Narrative
$comment: The prose-only, no-numbers rule is enforced authoritatively by engine/card_renderer.py:validate_narrative (issue #194 item 1). It rejects ASCII/Unicode digits AND spelled-out quantity claims in both languages (三成, 五萬, 兩倍, 百分之三十, thirty percent). ECMA-262 schema patterns cannot express the CJK/word-form rule, and the former per-field '^\D+$' matched ASCII digits only — it accepted full-width digits (３０) that the engine's Unicode \d rejects, so the two layers disagreed. The pattern is removed so this schema no longer makes a weaker, contradictory claim; card_renderer.validate_narrative is the single source of truth for digit-ban.

synthesis.description: Optional closing synthesis (#345): two to three sentences that condense the period's single most important cross-section judgment into one point of view, rendered as the card's final section, after Block 4 (Next step). It must connect facts that otherwise sit in separate sections (for example: concentration level + dollar exposure + what a drawdown would mean) into one conclusion — it must never restate a number, tile, or sentence that already appears elsewhere on the card. Omit the field entirely, rather than writing a placeholder, when nothing rises to that synthesis; the renderer drops the section with no header when the field is absent.

honesty.description: One qualitative sentence per triggered honesty-ledger key; preview fails unless every required_honesty_keys entry is covered (#82: conditions live in the engine, wording stays with the agent). Digit-ban is enforced by card_renderer.validate_narrative, not by a schema pattern (see top-level $comment).
